track.title: Historically Aligned Hard Track
track.description: Sixty-four unusual, cumulative systems problems that expand the runnable core toward the original transistor-to-browser ambition without claiming undocumented author intent or making unsafe physical work mandatory.

packs.title: Hostile C and Binary Forensics
packs.path: vault/Challenges/00 Hostile C and Binary Forensics.md
packs.overview: Turn the C runway into a forensic laboratory where formats, lifetimes, ABI boundaries, state machines, and build evidence must survive hostile inputs.
packs.entry_gate: Complete modules 00.01 through 00.08 and pass gate-00 before starting the boss.
packs.stage_handoff: A capsule-v1 replay format, libbyte artifact, minimized corpus, and reproducible-build manifest consumed by every later stage.
packs.safety: Run unknown bytes only through bounded parsers or the course machine; sanitizers are diagnostics, not a hostile-code sandbox.

sources.title: WG14 N2176 C17 draft
sources.use: Define integer, lifetime, representation, and undefined-behavior boundaries for every C artifact.

sources.title: System V ABI ELF specification
sources.use: Ground object, symbol, section, relocation, and ABI investigations in the executable-format contract.

sources.title: Clang AddressSanitizer documentation
sources.use: Configure reproducible memory-safety evidence without treating instrumentation as a proof.

sources.title: Reproducible Builds documentation
sources.use: Design two-root and two-time experiments and record every input affecting output hashes.

challenges.title: Binary Palimpsest
challenges.brief: Infer a mixed-endian checksummed format from specimens, then implement a strict C decoder and inverse encoder.
challenges.artifact: C codec, recovered schema, and specimen corpus
deliverables: A working C codec, recovered schema, and specimen corpus with reproducible build and invocation instructions.
A deterministic oracle or property suite plus a seeded adversarial regression corpus.
A replayable evidence packet with hashes, first divergence, invariant, and known limitation.
constraints: All required checks run offline with deterministic seeds and explicit resource bounds.
Implementation and oracle remain independently replaceable; fixed examples alone are insufficient.
Validate bounded data before mutation or output and keep raw evidence immutable.

acceptance.criterion: The artifact builds warning-clean and passes deterministic reference checks from a fresh workspace.
Every adversarial case replays and invalid input becomes a typed failure rather than a crash or hang.
Evidence records seed, tool versions, hashes, first divergence, invariant, and known limitation.
A downstream probe consumes the handoff without undocumented conversion or manual repair.

packs.title: Logic Under Cross-Examination
packs.overview: Build independent C and RTL models, then attack reset, timing, handshake, and waveform assumptions until the first divergent edge is reproducible.
packs.entry_gate: Complete modules 01.01 and 01.02, gate-00, and the H00 capsule handoff.
packs.stage_handoff: A simtrace-v1 edge schema, synthesizable clockwork block, C logic oracle, mutation report, and reset contract.
packs.safety: All required work is simulation-only; synthesis is optional and does not prove timing closure or electrical behavior.

sources.title: Verilator trace options

sources.title: AMD configurable logic guide UG574
sources.use: Relate LUT, carry, register, and control-set models to documented FPGA resources.

sources.title: cocotb timing model
sources.use: Define phases, edge observations, and race-free mixed-language checking.

sources.title: Yosys documentation
sources.use: Inspect synthesized structure while separating logical equivalence from source appearance.

packs.title: Bringup Through Hostile Wires
packs.overview: Treat LED timing, serial framing, MMIO side effects, FIFOs, and host bridges as unknown or adversarial systems.
packs.entry_gate: Complete modules 02.01 and 02.02, gate-02, and the H01 clockwork handoff.
packs.stage_handoff: A bus-v1 register contract, cycle-accurate UART, recovery console, and transaction corpus.
packs.safety: Required transport is mocked or loopback-only; do not attach unknown hardware or drive pins without an electrical plan.

packs.title: ISA CPU and Hostile Boot
packs.path: vault/Challenges/03 ISA CPU and Hostile Boot.md
packs.overview: Reconstruct instruction encodings, build independent execution models, mutate them, and carry an unknown ROM through hostile serial boot.
packs.entry_gate: Complete modules 03.01 through 03.03, gate-03, and the H02 recovery-console handoff.
packs.stage_handoff: A versioned ISA, assembler corpus, CPU trace ABI, boot monitor, and independently checked ROM repair.

packs.title: Network and Browser in an Adversarial Box

packs.overview: Reverse packet histories, attack TCP and process lifecycles, seal mappings, and fuzz HTTP and HTML before composing a hostile local Web.

challenges.artifact: TCP machine, sequence oracle, schedule corpus, and leak report
deliverables: A working TCP machine, sequence oracle, schedule corpus, and leak report with reproducible instructions.
An independent protocol or parser oracle plus seeded faults.
A replayable packet-to-process-to-render evidence packet.
constraints: All required networking is loopback or deterministic simulation.
Protocol and parser queues, states, inputs, and outputs are bounded.
Content bytes never become unescaped terminal control sequences.

acceptance.criterion: The artifact passes fresh deterministic protocol and parser checks.
Every adversarial packet or byte split replays without a crash, hang, or leak.
Evidence records seed, tools, hashes, first divergence, invariant, and limitation.
The next-stage probe consumes the browser and trace handoff directly.

packs.title: Evidence-First Physical Bringup
packs.overview: Reconstruct JTAG and flash protocols, audit board facts, diagnose clocks, and compare C, RTL, and recorded hardware without requiring a live board.
packs.entry_gate: Complete modules 07.01 through 07.03 or simulation equivalents, gate-07, and preserve the H06 browser workload.
packs.stage_handoff: A reviewed board pack, read-only flash transcript, three-way concordance report, rollback image, and bringup capsule.
packs.safety: Required work uses simulation and recordings; live work needs an allowlisted board, voltage checks, current limiting, stop rules, and experienced review.

sources.title: IEEE 1149.1 standard page
sources.use: Ground TAP, scan, reset, and boundary-scan terminology in the governing standard.

sources.title: AMD configuration guide UG470
sources.use: Define configuration, startup, reset, status, and handling for a supported FPGA.

sources.title: Digilent Arty A7 schematic
sources.use: Trace clock, reset, LED, JTAG, power, memory, and connector nets.

sources.title: AMD programming guide UG908
sources.use: Plan staged programming, capture evidence, and rollback with vendor tools.

challenges.title: TAP from Unknown State
challenges.brief: Model all TAP transitions, recover from any start, and generate shortest paths between every state pair.
challenges.artifact: C TAP model, path planner, exhaustive tests, and renderer
deliverables: A working C TAP model, path planner, exhaustive tests, and renderer with reproducible instructions.
A simulation or recorded-hardware oracle plus seeded fault fixtures.
A replayable bringup evidence packet with provenance and stop-go decisions.
constraints: Simulation and recorded fixtures satisfy every required acceptance check.
Board facts, tool facts, protocol hypotheses, and observations remain distinct.
Live programming or driving is disabled until the reviewed safety gate passes.

acceptance.criterion: The artifact passes fresh deterministic simulation and recorded-fixture checks.
Every injected physical-layer hypothesis replays with an earliest owning diagnosis.
Evidence records provenance, tools, hashes, first divergence, invariant, and limitation.
The final capsule consumes prior browser, machine, device, and logic handoffs directly.
